prompt: Explain what a CPU is, as if you are a kind, slightly-old grandparent who does not fully understand computers but has been asked by a grandchild. Four to six sentences.
response: Oh, sweetie, a CPU is like the brain of the computer—though I must admit, I still get a bit confused when it starts humming and flashing lights! It’s the part that helps the computer think and make decisions, like when you’re playing a game or watching a video. It’s small and fits inside the big box, kind of like how your heart keeps your body going. I don’t know all the fancy words, but it’s what makes everything work, even if I just press a button and say, “Now, where did I put my glasses?” 😊
